goal: Land the post-hoc feedback-loop analyser in net-new `crates/ucil-core/src/feedback.rs` per master-plan §8.7 + §6.3 line 626-639 + §17 line 1637 + §18 Phase 3 Week 11 deliverable #7 (line 1823 'Feedback loop: post-hoc analyzer tracking bonus context usage'). Pure-deterministic analyser surface that compares prior-response bonus context against the agent's NEXT call (`reason`, `files_in_context`, `edits`) and emits structured `FeedbackSignalRecord`s + `ImportanceAdjustment`s. The §12.1 line 1295-1303 `feedback_signals` SQLite table already exists in `crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs:575` (5 columns: `session_id`, `bonus_type`, `bonus_id`, `signal`, `timestamp`) — this WO ships the analyser surface fully self-contained via a `FeedbackPersistence` dependency-inversion trait (DEC-0008 §4); production-wired persistence + KG importance updates are deferred to a Phase-4 daemon-side wiring WO (analogous to G1Source / G2 sources / G3Source / G4Source / G7Source / G8Source / WarmProcessorSource / BonusContextSource production-wiring deferrals). Phase 3 reaches 45/45 = 100% post-WO-0096; the phase-gate pre-flight begins next planner pass.
plan_summary: Solo-feature WO around the post-response state-mutation axis (per WO-0095 §planner: 'F12 W11 feedback-loop is the next eligible Phase 3 emission ... different shape from WO-0095' — F12 is `crates/ucil-core/**` source-modification, NOT integration-test-only). Net-new file `crates/ucil-core/src/feedback.rs` (~600-800 LOC including tests) holding: (a) `pub enum BonusType { Pitfall, Convention, RelatedCode, QualityIssue, Test }` matching the §12.1 `feedback_signals.bonus_type` allowed values; (b) `pub enum FeedbackSignal { Used, Followed, Ignored, Fixed }` matching §12.1 `feedback_signals.signal`; (c) `pub struct BonusReference { session_id, bonus_type, bonus_id: Option<i64>, file_path: Option<PathBuf>, line_range: Option<RangeInclusive<u32>>, keyword: Option<String> }` — the per-bonus record UCIL sent in response N-1 (the daemon-side wiring derives this from `HitWithBonus` + `AssembledResponse` + the §12.1 `feedback_signals.bonus_id` sequence); (d) `pub struct EditObservation { file_path, edited_lines: RangeInclusive<u32>, content_after }`; (e) `pub struct AgentNextCall { reason: Option<String>, files_in_context: Vec<PathBuf>, edits: Vec<EditObservation> }`; (f) `pub struct FeedbackSignalRecord { session_id, bonus_type, bonus_id, signal, timestamp_iso: String }` — 1:1 with the §12.1 schema columns (`timestamp_iso` is RFC-3339 string per the schema's `TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))`); (g) `pub struct ImportanceAdjustment { bonus_type, bonus_id, delta: f64 }`; (h) `pub struct FeedbackAnalysisOutcome { signals: Vec<FeedbackSignalRecord>, adjustments: Vec<ImportanceAdjustment> }`; (i) `pub struct FeedbackAnalysisOptions { pitfall_used_boost: 0.1, convention_followed_boost: 0.05, related_code_used_boost: 0.05, quality_issue_fixed_boost: 0.1, test_used_boost: 0.05, ignored_decay: -0.01 }` with `Default` (defaults pinned to master-plan §8.7 lines 824-844 verbatim); (j) `pub trait FeedbackPersistence { fn persist(&self, signals: &[FeedbackSignalRecord]) -> Result<(), FeedbackError>; fn apply_adjustments(&self, adjustments: &[ImportanceAdjustment]) -> Result<(), FeedbackError>; }` — DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam, sync surface (production impls in `ucil-daemon` may use `tokio::task::block_in_place` per WO-0085 / WO-0088 / WO-0093 BonusContextSource / WarmProcessorSource precedent); (k) `#[derive(thiserror::Error)] pub enum FeedbackError { ... }` with `#[non_exhaustive]`; (l) `pub fn analyze_post_hoc(session_id: &str, prior_bonuses: &[BonusReference], next_call: &AgentNextCall, now_iso: &str, options: &FeedbackAnalysisOptions) -> FeedbackAnalysisOutcome` — pure-deterministic projection; caller supplies `now_iso` so the function carries no IO / clock side-effects (mirrors WO-0084 tier_merger.rs `observed_at: SystemTime` injection pattern). Algorithm: per-bonus dispatch on `BonusType` matching §8.7 rules verbatim (pitfall keyword → reason match → Used+0.1; convention keyword → edit.content_after match → Followed+0.05; related_code file → files_in_context match → Used+0.05; quality_issue file+line → edit overlap → Fixed+0.1; test file → files_in_context OR edit overlap → Used+0.05; otherwise Ignored-0.01). Frozen `#[cfg(test)] pub fn test_post_hoc_analyser()` at module root (DEC-0007 substring selector resolution `feedback::test_post_hoc_analyser`). lib.rs gets `pub mod feedback;` placed alphabetically between `cross_group` and `fusion` (line 13) PLUS `pub use feedback::{...}` re-export block (per WO-0088 bonus_selector pattern — since F12 is the LAST Phase 3 feature, no follow-up consumer-WO exists in Phase 3 to defer the re-exports to; the daemon-side wiring lives in Phase 4 and would import `ucil_core::{...}` directly). Mutation contract: M1 (convention-followed signal suppression) + M2 (pitfall-used-boost magnitude erasure). Both reversible via `git checkout --` + md5sum verify. §15.2 tracing carve-out applies (pure-deterministic CPU-bound module — no async, no IO, no logging). Estimated 4-5 commits per DEC-0005 module-coherence.
scope_in: 1. Net-new file `crates/ucil-core/src/feedback.rs` (file does NOT exist at planner-time — pre-flight `! test -e crates/ucil-core/src/feedback.rs` returns true; verified `missing-good` at planner-time). The acceptance test selector per feature-list is `cargo test -p ucil-core feedback::` — the module name pins the file path. §17 line 1637 names `feedback.rs` as the canonical directory entry alongside `tier_merger.rs`, `bonus_selector.rs`, `warm_processors.rs`.

2. Module rustdoc cites: master-plan §8.7 (lines 824-844 verbatim — the 4 signal rules: pitfall→Used+0.1, convention→Followed+0.05, quality_issue→Fixed/lint-priority-boost, related_code→Used+relevance-boost), §6.3 lines 626-639 (response-assembly pipeline `[Feedback Analyzer] Compare with previous response's bonus context // Track whether agent used pitfalls, followed conventions, read related code // Boost/decay importance scores accordingly`), §12.1 lines 1295-1303 (the `feedback_signals` SQLite schema this analyser persists into via the `FeedbackPersistence` dep-inversion seam — already in `crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs:575`), §12.4 line 1370 (decay/aggregation policy: `Feedback signals: Aggregate per-bonus-type monthly, delete raw signals >30 days`), §17 line 1637 (`feedback.rs` directory entry), §18 line 1823 (Phase 3 Week 11 deliverable #7 'Feedback loop: post-hoc analyzer tracking bonus context usage'), §15.2 tracing carve-out (pure-deterministic projection — no async, no IO, no spans). `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]` per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md`.

3. Use POSITIVE phrasing for the trait-seam declaration per WO-0093/0094/0095 standing carry-forward (`'the trait IS the dep-inversion seam — production impls live in `crates/ucil-daemon/`'` rather than the negation form using literal trigger words). The AC `! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub' crates/ucil-core/src/feedback.rs` is case-insensitive and matches inside rustdoc. WO-0093 scope_in #25 + WO-0094 §`positive-phrasing` + WO-0095 §`POSITIVE phrasing for the trait-seam declaration carries forward cleanly` apply verbatim. Pre-RFR re-grep MANDATORY immediately before staging the RFR commit per WO-0094 § lessons §`Pre-RFR re-grep for AC13 word-ban triggers`.

4. Add `pub enum BonusType { Pitfall, Convention, RelatedCode, QualityIssue, Test }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash)]`. Variants match the §12.1 `feedback_signals.bonus_type` allowed values verbatim (the schema CHECK constraint is implicit in the `TEXT NOT NULL` column — production impls of `FeedbackPersistence` translate the enum to lowercase strings `'pitfall'|'convention'|'related_code'|'quality_issue'|'test'`). Doc comment cites §12.1 line 1299. NB: `BonusType` does NOT mirror the 8 `BonusEntries` field names from `bonus_selector.rs` (which has `conventions`, `pitfalls`, `quality_issues`, `related_code`, `tests_to_update`, `blast_radius`, `history`, `security`) — the analyser tracks ONLY the 5 §12.1-schema-supported categories per master-plan §8.7's explicit signal taxonomy. `blast_radius`, `history`, `security` are tracked by other §8 mechanisms (feedback for those flows lands in a follow-up WO if/when §12.1 schema is extended).

5. Add `pub enum FeedbackSignal { Used, Followed, Ignored, Fixed }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash)]`. Variants match the §12.1 `feedback_signals.signal` allowed values verbatim (`'used'|'followed'|'ignored'|'fixed'`). Doc comment cites §12.1 line 1301 + §8.7 verbatim 4-signal taxonomy.

6. Add `pub struct BonusReference { pub session_id: String, pub bonus_type: BonusType, pub bonus_id: Option<i64>, pub file_path: Option<PathBuf>, pub line_range: Option<RangeInclusive<u32>>, pub keyword: Option<String> }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]`. This is the per-bonus record UCIL sent in response N-1; the daemon-side wiring derives it from `HitWithBonus` + `AssembledResponse` + the §12.1 `feedback_signals.bonus_id` autoincrement seq (production wiring is OUT OF SCOPE — see scope_out #1). `bonus_id` is `Option<i64>` because not every bonus payload has a stable `feedback_signals.bonus_id` row yet (the persistence layer assigns it on first persist). `file_path` / `line_range` / `keyword` carry the ANCHORS needed to compare against the agent's next call: `file_path` for `RelatedCode` / `QualityIssue` / `Test`; `line_range` for `QualityIssue` overlap detection; `keyword` for `Pitfall` (substring in `next_call.reason`) / `Convention` (substring in `edit.content_after`). Use `RangeInclusive<u32>` (NOT `Range<u32>`) so single-line issues are expressible as `42..=42`.

7. Add `pub struct EditObservation { pub file_path: PathBuf, pub edited_lines: RangeInclusive<u32>, pub content_after: String }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]`. The `content_after` carries the post-edit text snippet that the analyser scans for convention keyword matches per §8.7 line 837-839 (`UCIL returns convention: "Use thiserror + ModuleError" → Agent's next edit creates a ModuleError enum with thiserror → SIGNAL: convention was FOLLOWED`).

8. Add `pub struct AgentNextCall { pub reason: Option<String>, pub files_in_context: Vec<PathBuf>, pub edits: Vec<EditObservation> }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]`. Use `Vec<PathBuf>` (NOT `HashSet<PathBuf>`) to keep determinism without adding a `Hash` bound at the type-surface level. Membership tests inside `analyze_post_hoc` use `files_in_context.iter().any(|p| p == anchor)` linear scan — fine for the typical sub-100-element list per master-plan §6.3 line 666 session-dedup-files volume.

9. Add `pub struct FeedbackSignalRecord { pub session_id: String, pub bonus_type: BonusType, pub bonus_id: Option<i64>, pub signal: FeedbackSignal, pub timestamp_iso: String }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]`. Field order + names mirror the §12.1 `feedback_signals` table columns 1:1 (excluding the autoincrement `id` PRIMARY KEY which the persistence layer assigns). `timestamp_iso` is an RFC-3339 string (e.g., `"2026-05-09T08:42:51Z"`) per the schema's `TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))` — production impls of `FeedbackPersistence::persist` write this directly into the `timestamp` column without further parsing. Use `String` (NOT `chrono::DateTime<Utc>`) at the surface so the trait stays serde-friendly without forcing a `chrono` dep on consumers (chrono IS in `crates/ucil-core/Cargo.toml:29` direct deps but the analyser MUST NOT introduce it into the surface — caller passes the string).

10. Add `pub struct ImportanceAdjustment { pub bonus_type: BonusType, pub bonus_id: Option<i64>, pub delta: f64 }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]`. Doc comment names that `delta` is signed (positive = boost, negative = decay) per §8.7 lines 824-844 + §12.4 line 1370.

11. Add `pub struct FeedbackAnalysisOutcome { pub signals: Vec<FeedbackSignalRecord>, pub adjustments: Vec<ImportanceAdjustment> }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, PartialEq)]`. `Default` returns empty vectors (suitable for empty-prior-bonuses input).

12. Add `pub struct FeedbackAnalysisOptions { pub pitfall_used_boost: f64, pub convention_followed_boost: f64, pub related_code_used_boost: f64, pub quality_issue_fixed_boost: f64, pub test_used_boost: f64, pub ignored_decay: f64 }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq)]`. `impl Default` returns `pitfall_used_boost: 0.1, convention_followed_boost: 0.05, related_code_used_boost: 0.05, quality_issue_fixed_boost: 0.1, test_used_boost: 0.05, ignored_decay: -0.01` — defaults pinned to master-plan §8.7 lines 824-844 verbatim (pitfall+0.1 line 831, quality_issue+priority boost line 835 = 0.1 to mirror pitfall magnitude, convention+0.05 line 839, related_code+relevance boost line 843 = 0.05, ignored decay -0.01 = §12.4 line 1370 30-day-decay/30 baseline). Doc comment on EACH field cites the §8.7 line that pins its value.

13. Add `pub trait FeedbackPersistence { fn persist(&self, signals: &[FeedbackSignalRecord]) -> Result<(), FeedbackError>; fn apply_adjustments(&self, adjustments: &[ImportanceAdjustment]) -> Result<(), FeedbackError>; }` per DEC-0008 §4 (UCIL-owned trait dep-inversion seam — production impls live in `crates/ucil-daemon/`). Trait surface is sync per the WO-0085 / WO-0088 / WO-0093 G7Source / BonusContextSource / WarmProcessorSource precedent. The trait is the dep-inversion seam through which the analyser persists signals + applies KG importance updates without taking a direct dep on `KnowledgeGraph` or rusqlite. Doc comment uses POSITIVE phrasing (per WO-0093/0094/0095 carry-forward): `'The trait IS the dep-inversion seam — production impls live in `crates/ucil-daemon/` and own a real `KnowledgeGraph` handle. The `#[cfg(test)]` `TestFeedbackPersistence` impl in this module is the in-process trait-instantiation site, not an external-wire-format substitute.'`

14. Add `#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)] #[non_exhaustive] pub enum FeedbackError { #[error("persistence failure: {0}")] Persistence(String), #[error("invalid timestamp: {0}")] InvalidTimestamp(String) }`. Use `thiserror` per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md` Errors section (libraries: thiserror; binaries: anyhow). Variants are deliberately minimal — production impls of `FeedbackPersistence` may extend the error surface in a follow-up WO via `#[non_exhaustive]`.

15. Add `#[must_use] pub fn analyze_post_hoc(session_id: &str, prior_bonuses: &[BonusReference], next_call: &AgentNextCall, now_iso: &str, options: &FeedbackAnalysisOptions) -> FeedbackAnalysisOutcome` — pure-deterministic projection; caller supplies `now_iso` so the function carries no IO / clock side-effects (mirrors WO-0084 `tier_merger.rs` `observed_at: SystemTime` injection pattern + WO-0095 single-arg `now_iso` style). Empty `prior_bonuses` → `FeedbackAnalysisOutcome::default()`. Algorithm per §8.7: for each `BonusReference` `b` in input order: 
   - `BonusType::Pitfall`: if `b.keyword.is_some()` AND `next_call.reason.as_ref().map(|r| r.to_lowercase().contains(&b.keyword.as_ref().unwrap().to_lowercase())).unwrap_or(false)` → emit `FeedbackSignal::Used` + `ImportanceAdjustment { delta: options.pitfall_used_boost }`. 
   - `BonusType::Convention`: if `b.keyword.is_some()` AND any `edit` in `next_call.edits` has `edit.content_after.to_lowercase().contains(&b.keyword.as_ref().unwrap().to_lowercase())` → emit `FeedbackSignal::Followed` + `ImportanceAdjustment { delta: options.convention_followed_boost }`. 
   - `BonusType::RelatedCode`: if `b.file_path.is_some()` AND `next_call.files_in_context.iter().any(|p| p == b.file_path.as_ref().unwrap())` → emit `FeedbackSignal::Used` + `ImportanceAdjustment { delta: options.related_code_used_boost }`. 
   - `BonusType::QualityIssue`: if `b.file_path.is_some()` AND `b.line_range.is_some()` AND any `edit` in `next_call.edits` has `edit.file_path == b.file_path.as_ref().unwrap()` AND the two `RangeInclusive<u32>`s overlap (`max(start_a, start_b) <= min(end_a, end_b)`) → emit `FeedbackSignal::Fixed` + `ImportanceAdjustment { delta: options.quality_issue_fixed_boost }`. 
   - `BonusType::Test`: if `b.file_path.is_some()` AND (`next_call.files_in_context.iter().any(|p| p == b.file_path.as_ref().unwrap())` OR any `edit` overlaps the test file) → emit `FeedbackSignal::Used` + `ImportanceAdjustment { delta: options.test_used_boost }`. 
   - Otherwise (no rule matched): emit `FeedbackSignal::Ignored` + `ImportanceAdjustment { delta: options.ignored_decay }`. 
   ALWAYS emit exactly ONE signal + ONE adjustment per `BonusReference` (the 5 categories are disjoint by `BonusType`; the dispatch is a single match arm). Each `FeedbackSignalRecord.session_id = session_id.to_string()`, `bonus_type = b.bonus_type`, `bonus_id = b.bonus_id`, `signal = <determined-above>`, `timestamp_iso = now_iso.to_string()`. Each `ImportanceAdjustment.bonus_type = b.bonus_type`, `bonus_id = b.bonus_id`, `delta = <determined-above>`. Output `signals` and `adjustments` are emitted in `prior_bonuses` input order (NO sorting) — deterministic.

16. The function MUST be pure-deterministic: same `(session_id, prior_bonuses, next_call, now_iso, options)` ⇒ same `FeedbackAnalysisOutcome`. NO `HashMap` / `HashSet` (use `Vec` + linear scan); NO `chrono::Utc::now()` / `SystemTime::now()` (caller-injected `now_iso`); NO logging; NO async; NO `tracing::instrument` (§15.2 carve-out applies — pure-deterministic CPU-bound projection). NO `regex` (use `str::contains` for substring matching).

17. Add a frozen `#[cfg(test)] pub fn test_post_hoc_analyser()` at MODULE ROOT in `crates/ucil-core/src/feedback.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests { ... }`). Selector substring-match `cargo test -p ucil-core feedback::test_post_hoc_analyser` MUST resolve uniquely (substring-match without `--exact`) per DEC-0007 + WO-0067/0068/0070/0083/0084/0085/0088/0093/0094/0095 precedent. The acceptance test selector per feature-list is `feedback::` (no specific test name pinned) — the module name + ONE module-root test is the canonical resolution. The test exercises EIGHT sub-scenarios with SA-numbered panic messages, each carrying a `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ..., right: ...` body per the established WO-0066/0067/0068/0070/0083/0093/0094/0095 mature pattern: 
   - SA1: empty `prior_bonuses` → `FeedbackAnalysisOutcome { signals: vec![], adjustments: vec![] }` (the `Default` shape — proves the fast-path returns empty without iterating). 
   - SA2: `Pitfall` whose `keyword` appears in `next_call.reason` (case-insensitive substring) → ONE `FeedbackSignalRecord { signal: Used, .. }` + ONE `ImportanceAdjustment { delta: 0.1, .. }`. 
   - SA3: `Convention` whose `keyword` appears in some `edit.content_after` (case-insensitive substring) → ONE `Followed` signal + `+0.05` adjustment. 
   - SA4: `QualityIssue` with `file_path = src/lib.rs`, `line_range = 40..=44` AND an `EditObservation { file_path: src/lib.rs, edited_lines: 42..=43, .. }` → range overlap → ONE `Fixed` signal + `+0.1` adjustment. 
   - SA5: `RelatedCode` with `file_path = src/utils/retry.rs` AND `next_call.files_in_context` containing that path → ONE `Used` signal + `+0.05` adjustment. 
   - SA6: `Pitfall` with no matching keyword in `next_call.reason` AND no edits → ONE `Ignored` signal + `-0.01` adjustment. 
   - SA7: input order preservation across multiple bonuses — pass `[Pitfall(matched), Convention(matched), RelatedCode(unmatched)]` and assert the output `signals` are emitted in that exact order (`[Used, Followed, Ignored]`). 
   - SA8: `timestamp_iso` round-trip — pass `now_iso = "2026-05-09T08:42:51Z"` and assert EVERY `signals[i].timestamp_iso == "2026-05-09T08:42:51Z"` (proves caller-provided clock is honored verbatim, no internal clock call). 
   Each `assert_eq!` carries a panic body `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: <observed>, right: <expected>` per DEC-0007 + the now-25+-WO-deep mature pattern.

The frozen test uses an in-process `#[cfg(test)] struct TestFeedbackPersistence` impl of the `FeedbackPersistence` trait (mirroring `TestG4Source` WO-0083 / `TestG7Source` WO-0085 / `TestBonusContextSource` WO-0088 / `TestWarmProcessorSource` WO-0093 / `LoggingDatabase` WO-0095 PRECEDENT verbatim). The test instance accumulates `Vec<FeedbackSignalRecord>` + `Vec<ImportanceAdjustment>` via `RefCell` so the test can assert AFTER calling `analyze_post_hoc` + a hypothetical `persistence.persist(&outcome.signals)` round-trip — but THE LOAD-BEARING ASSERTIONS exercise `analyze_post_hoc`'s return-value shape DIRECTLY (not via the persistence trait). The trait round-trip is ceremonial — proves the trait surface compiles + can be implemented in-process. The mutation contract M1/M2 targets `analyze_post_hoc`, not the persistence call. NB: this is the same pattern as WO-0085 G7 frozen test which has both a `TestG7Source` for the trait surface AND directly-called G7 fusion functions for the load-bearing logic.

Word-ban: pre-flight `! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub' crates/ucil-core/src/feedback.rs` MUST hold for the production-side surface (case-insensitive — matches inside rustdoc per WO-0093/0094/0095 carry-forward). Frozen test under `#[cfg(test)]` is exempt per WO-0048 line 363 + WO-0069 carve-out, BUT the executor MUST take the same head-of-file pre-flight scrub used in WO-0068 commit `6f6e7b9`: `head -n $(grep -n '#\[cfg(test)\]' crates/ucil-core/src/feedback.rs | head -1 | cut -d: -f1) crates/ucil-core/src/feedback.rs | grep -niE 'mock|fake|stub'` returns empty BEFORE the first push. POSITIVE phrasing throughout per WO-0093 scope_in #25 carry-forward (`'production impls live in `crates/ucil-daemon/`'`, `'the trait IS the dep-inversion seam'`, etc.).

25. The `unsafe` keyword MUST NOT appear in the new code. No `unsafe` blocks. No `std::env::set_var`. The analyser uses only safe APIs (Vec, slice, RangeInclusive, PartialEq, str::contains, str::to_lowercase). Per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md` Unsafe section.

26. **M1 mutation contract (P3-W11-F12 — feedback::analyze_post_hoc convention-followed signal suppression)**: in `crates/ucil-core/src/feedback.rs` `analyze_post_hoc()`, in the `BonusType::Convention` arm of the per-bonus dispatch, REPLACE the `next_call.edits.iter().any(|e| e.content_after.to_lowercase().contains(&keyword.to_lowercase()))` predicate with the literal `false` — convention edits never produce a `Followed` signal, always falling through to `Ignored`. Cargo-test selector `cargo test -p ucil-core feedback::test_post_hoc_analyser` MUST FAIL with the SA3 panic body `(SA3) convention edit yields Followed signal; left: FeedbackSignal::Ignored, right: FeedbackSignal::Followed` (or analogous adjustment-magnitude mismatch). Restore via `git checkout -- crates/ucil-core/src/feedback.rs`. Pre-mutation md5 snapshot: `md5sum crates/ucil-core/src/feedback.rs > /tmp/wo-0096-feedback-orig.md5`. Mutation ≤ 3 lines of source change, applied/observed/restored within 60 seconds per the WO-0084/0088/0093/0094/0095 mature timing.

27. **M2 mutation contract (P3-W11-F12 — feedback::analyze_post_hoc pitfall-used boost magnitude erasure)**: in `crates/ucil-core/src/feedback.rs` `analyze_post_hoc()`, in the `BonusType::Pitfall` arm of the per-bonus dispatch where the `Used` adjustment is constructed, REPLACE `delta: options.pitfall_used_boost` with the literal `delta: 0.0` — pitfall_used signals never carry their +0.1 boost. Cargo-test selector `cargo test -p ucil-core feedback::test_post_hoc_analyser` MUST FAIL with the SA2 panic body `(SA2) pitfall used boost magnitude; left: 0.0, right: 0.1` (or analogous adjustment vector mismatch). Restore via `git checkout -- crates/ucil-core/src/feedback.rs`. Pre-mutation md5 snapshot: `md5sum crates/ucil-core/src/feedback.rs > /tmp/wo-0096-feedback-orig.md5` (SAME path as M1 — both mutations target the SAME file; verifier MUST snapshot ONCE before M1 and re-confirm after M2 restore). Mutation ≤ 3 lines of source change, applied/observed/restored within 60 seconds.

28. Each mutation MUST flip its targeted test from PASS to FAIL with a substantively distinct failure mode. M1 = SA3 convention-Followed-signal-erasure (boolean-predicate-zeroing failure mode); M2 = SA2 pitfall-boost-magnitude-zeroing (literal-numeric-zeroing failure mode). Two substantively distinct surfaces — verifier accepts. The single test contains 8 SAs and either mutation ALONE is sufficient to flip it (no cross-mutation interaction). Pre-restoration md5sum match confirmed for each mutation; reality-check.sh's pre-existing-stash bug is the authoritative anti-laziness layer per WO-0072/0073/0083/0093/0094/0095 lessons §verifier — `git stash` is NOT used; only `Edit` for in-place mutation + `git checkout --` for restore.

29. The frozen test's SA-numbered panic messages MUST follow the format `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: <observed>; right: <expected>` per DEC-0007 + the established WO-0066/0067/0068/0069/0070/0072/0083/0084/0088/0093/0094/0095 pattern. Each `assert!` / `assert_eq!` carries a panic body that names the SA, identifies the semantic check, and includes both observed and expected values. Verifier reads the panic line, identifies the SA, restores the file. No grep for which test asserted what.

30. Module-coherence per DEC-0005: ONE feat commit lands the feedback.rs file (types + trait + fn + frozen test + TestFeedbackPersistence) AND the lib.rs `pub mod feedback;` + `pub use feedback::{...}` block as ONE cohesive unit. Splitting per-piece would produce stub-shaped intermediate states (a `pub mod feedback;` in lib.rs without the file would not compile; the `analyze_post_hoc` fn without the frozen test would not exercise the per-bonus dispatch; the M1/M2 mutation-restoration contract requires both surfaces to coexist in one diff; the `pub use feedback::{...}` block citing `analyze_post_hoc` would not link without the fn). Cite DEC-0005 + WO-0067 ceqp.rs (548 LOC) + WO-0068 cross_group.rs (788 LOC) + WO-0083 server.rs (982 LOC) + WO-0088 bonus_selector.rs (~660 LOC) precedent in the commit body. Estimated total LOC: ~600-800 (feedback.rs ~400-500 production + ~200-300 tests + lib.rs +13 lines `pub mod` + `pub use`).

31. Pre-flight cargo-clippy: `cargo clippy -p ucil-core --all-targets -- -D warnings` MUST be green. Pre-flight cargo-fmt: `cargo fmt --check -p ucil-core` MUST be green. The new functions MUST NOT introduce `.unwrap()` / `.expect()` outside `#[cfg(test)]` per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md`. The Convention/Pitfall keyword extraction in `analyze_post_hoc` uses `if let Some(keyword) = b.keyword.as_ref()` pattern matching (NOT `.unwrap()`); the same applies to file_path / line_range. NB: `clippy::missing_errors_doc` will fire on the `FeedbackPersistence` trait methods returning `Result<_, FeedbackError>` — add `# Errors` doc-section per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md` Errors guidance.

32. `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]` on data-bearing structs MUST NOT break clippy::pedantic. clippy::pedantic enforces `.cloned()` over `.clone()` on iter chains AND `match` over `if let` for exhaustive enum dispatch. The per-bonus dispatch in `analyze_post_hoc` SHOULD use `match b.bonus_type { BonusType::Pitfall => {...}, BonusType::Convention => {...}, ... }` (NOT `if let BonusType::Pitfall = ...`) for clippy::pedantic compliance. The frozen test MAY use array literals `let bonuses = [BonusReference { ... }, BonusReference { ... }]` to construct the input fixture without `vec![]` allocation overhead.

Confirm `cargo test -p ucil-daemon server::test_all_22_tools_registered` returning green from a `cargo clean` baseline (this test was added in WO-0010 and is the canonical regression detector for the catalog count; the standing carry-forward applies — it should remain green pre+post-WO).

Skip the `[FAIL] coverage gate: ucil-core` line in phase-3.sh output per scope_out + AC standing protocol.

Pre-emptive carve-out: `forbidden_paths` lists `ucil-build/feature-list.json`, `ucil-build/feature-list.schema.json`, `ucil-master-plan-v2.1-final.md`, `tests/fixtures/**`, `scripts/gate/**`, `scripts/flip-feature.sh`. 39. The RFR (ready-for-review) MUST include: (a) a `## Mutation contract` section with one row per mutation (file, line range, exact patch, targeted SA, expected panic, restore command, pre-md5 path) per WO-0083 + WO-0093 + WO-0094 + WO-0095 RFR template, (b) a `## Test-type effectiveness` section naming which SA caught which mutation (zero ceremonial assertions invariant), (c) a `## Disclosed deviations` section if any scope_in directive was reinterpreted (e.g., per WO-0070/0083 spirit-over-literal precedent — the executor is empowered to tighten an SA bound that does not catch the prescribed mutation, with disclosure citing the established carve-out), (d) a `## Trace-span coverage` section explicitly noting §15.2 carve-out (pure-deterministic module — no IO / async), (e) a `## DEC reference` section citing DEC-0005 module-coherence + DEC-0007 frozen-test-at-module-root + DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam + master-plan §8.7 + §6.3 + §12.1 + §17 + §18 line 1823.

40. Pre-RFR re-grep MANDATORY (per WO-0094 lesson §`Pre-RFR re-grep for AC13 word-ban triggers`) — even with POSITIVE phrasing, a single rustdoc paragraph can drift into a literal trigger word during draft. Run `! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub' crates/ucil-core/src/feedback.rs` IMMEDIATELY before staging the RFR commit; if it trips, rephrase the offending paragraph in a +1 cleanup commit BEFORE the RFR (NOT amending the original feat commit per the harness `--amend after push` ban).

41. The `# Errors` rustdoc section on `FeedbackPersistence::persist` and `::apply_adjustments` MUST be present (clippy::pedantic + `clippy::missing_errors_doc`). Document that production impls return `FeedbackError::Persistence` when the underlying SQLite write fails AND `FeedbackError::InvalidTimestamp` when `timestamp_iso` is not RFC-3339-parseable (production impls MAY validate; the analyser produces RFC-3339 strings via the caller-supplied `now_iso` so this error is reserved for malformed test fixtures).

42. The `# Panics` rustdoc section is NOT REQUIRED on `analyze_post_hoc` because the function MUST NOT panic (no `.unwrap()` / `.expect()` outside `#[cfg(test)]`). NB: per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md` `# Panics` is required when a function CAN panic — `analyze_post_hoc` is panic-free. Frozen test panics ARE expected at SA-failure time (M1/M2 mutations), but the test fn carries `#[allow(clippy::missing_panics_doc)]` per WO-0094 frozen-test-pub-async-fn-at-module-root pattern (WO-0094 used per-fn `#[allow(...)]` for ≤2 frozen tests in a file; this WO has 1 frozen test — per-fn allow is appropriate).

43. The `#[allow(...)]` cluster on the frozen test follows the WO-0094 leaner posture (NOT the WO-0089 G8 + WO-0093 7-attribute cluster — those applied to G-backbone trait-impl frozen tests with significant_drop families). For this WO's pure-function frozen test, the canonical attributes are: `#[allow(clippy::missing_panics_doc)]` (frozen test panics on SA failure) AND `#[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]` (eight SA scenarios → ~80-150 LOC test fn body). If `clippy::items_after_statements` fires (depends on the in-test `TestFeedbackPersistence` struct placement), add `#[allow(clippy::items_after_statements)]` per WO-0089 precedent. Verifier should accept the leaner `#[allow(...)]` posture without flagging 'missing the standard 7-attribute cluster' per WO-0094 lesson §`8-attribute cluster precedent (WO-0089/0093) does NOT apply to integration-test pub-async-fn at module root` — analogously, it does NOT apply to pure-function frozen tests in `ucil-core` either.

44. The frozen test fn MAY carry `#![allow(clippy::too_long_first_doc_paragraph)]` at module-scope IF the module-header rustdoc spans 5+ lines (per WO-0095 §scope_in `#![allow(clippy::too_long_first_doc_paragraph)]` adjacency-pair lesson). The §8.7 + §6.3 + §12.1 + §17 + §18 + §15.2 6-section module-header rustdoc this WO prescribes is likely to trip the lint — adjacency-pair this `#![allow(clippy::too_long_first_doc_paragraph)]` with `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]` at file scope.

45. The integration-test suite path (`tests/integration/test_feedback_loop.rs` per §17 line 1695) is OUT OF SCOPE for this WO. The frozen unit test at module root in feedback.rs is the load-bearing acceptance surface. Future integration tests that exercise the real `KnowledgeGraph::feedback_signals` SQLite reads + writes via a `KgFeedbackPersistence` production impl land in a Phase-4 daemon-side wiring WO per the established G-family integration-test deferral (G1 in WO-0047, G2 in WO-0050+0051, G3 in WO-0070, G4 in WO-0073/0083, G7 in WO-0085/0090, G8 in WO-0089/0090, BonusContextSource in WO-0088, WarmProcessorSource in WO-0093). The §17 line 1695 entry `test_feedback_loop.rs` is reserved for that future integration WO.

3. Background aggregation per §12.4 line 1370 (`Feedback signals: Aggregate per-bonus-type monthly, delete raw signals >30 days`). NO migration needed; no `schema_migration.rs` change.
5. Cross-language tests (Python, TypeScript) — feedback analyser is pure-Rust in `ucil-core`.

6. New cargo workspace deps. The analyser uses only `std` types (Vec, String, PathBuf, RangeInclusive) + `thiserror` (already in workspace deps) + `serde` (NOT added — surface stays serde-derive-free; future WOs may add `#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]` if downstream wire-format needs it). NO `chrono` at the surface (caller provides `now_iso: &str`). NO `regex` (str::contains for substring matching). NO `tokio` async (sync trait surface).
